expect: all five files are actually read — the photo LOOKED AT (vision), the voice memo TRANSCRIBED, the PDF and the spreadsheet PARSED
it OFFERS to organize this without being asked, naming at least two real specifics of mine (a place, a date, a booking)
it builds NOTHING yet — no spaces, no tables, no pages, no app (the offer precedes any authoring yield)

turns.sent: Ok this is getting out of hand. I've got the whole Tanzania trip spread across about six different places — a notes file, a spreadsheet with the costs, the crater park-fee PDF, a photo I liked from Stone Town, and a voice note I left myself at the crater. Attaching all of it. I don't trust myself to keep it straight on my phone once we're actually there — can you help me get on top of it?
turns.lastText: {"type":"Stack","props":{"gap":3},"children":[{"type":"Heading","props":{"level":2},"children":["Here's what I found across all your material"]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["You've got a rich spread — trip notes covering the full itinerary (Egypt → Arusha → safari circuit → Zanzibar → Dar), a detailed cost spreadsheet, the official Ngorongoro park-fee tariff sheet, your Stone Town photo, and the crater-day voice memo with Emmanuel's ranger-tip heads-up and the bracelet purchase."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["This is exactly the kind of thing that deserves to live in ONE place — an app you can open on your phone with the costs, the itinerary, the key contacts, and the per-topic details (park rules, tipping norms, what you bought where). Want me to pull all of this together into an app right here, organized and searchable?"]}]}
